steps: Carry out the PCR process under the UV light cabinet. Prepare a Working Dilution ( 25 micromolar (µM) ) of the PCR primers. A B C D 1 PCR primers Sequence 5' - 3' Stock Working 2 Forward primer CTG CAG CTT CGA GAG GAA AG 100 µM 25 µM 3 Reverse primer (WT/Flox) CAC TCT GTC CTC AGG CCT TC 100 µM 25 µM 4 Reverse primer 1 (KO) TGA GAA GTG GGA ATC GGG 100 µM 25 µM

Prepare a mix for all samples containing the following components: Prepare positive and negative controls per gene and prepare extra volume to account for pipetting variation. A B C 1 Component Amount Final conce 2 F primer (25 µM) 0.75 µl 750 nM 3 R primer (25 µM) 0.75 µl 750 nM 4 R primer 1 (25 µM) 0.75 µl 750 nM 5 PCR mix (2x) 12.5 µl 1x 6 H2O 8.75 µl -- 7 DNA 1.5 µl 8 Total volume 25 µl
To each PCR tube, add 1.5 µL gDNA from each sample and 23.5 µL from the reaction mixture.
Run PCR protocol A B C D 1 Initial denaturation 94 °C 3' 2 Denaturation 94 °C 30'' X30 3 Annealing 58 °C 30" 4 Elongation 72 °C 30" 5 Final extension 72°C 10' 6 Hold 4 °C 

Prepare a 1.5% agarose gel in TBE buffer containing Midori Green (1:10000). Double gel: A B 1 TBE 250 ml 2 Agarose powder 3.75 g 3 Midori Green 25 µl Single gel: A B 1 TBE 150 ml 2 Agarose powder 2.25 g 3 Midori Green 15 µl
Dilute TBE from the stock (10x) to a 1x in distilled water.
Weight and add the agarose powder in a dedicated glass bottle together with TBE.
Heat the solution in the microwave.
Cool it down by swirling underneath cold water.
Add the Midori Green nucleic acid staining solution and mix it again.
Pour it in the gel system and remove the bubbles (using a tip). Insert the combs and wait for its gelification (+/- 20 min).
Place the gel in the system and cover with TBE buffer.

Vortex ladder prior to loading to ensure even glycerol distribution. Load the samples ( 25 µL ) + DNA ladder ( 15 µL ) into one well of the gel . Do not leave more than 4 wells without any sample to avoid the smiling effect.
Run the samples for 01:20:00 at 120 V. Black is negative, red is positive. (The DNA is negatively charged and will run towards the positive electrode.) Always run to red!

Visualize the gel in the machine Gene Flash (Syngene Bio Imaging; Westburg, VWR) OR via Bio-Rad Chemidoc MP Imaging System. A B C 1 Results Band Notation 2 Floxed 564bp Flox 3 Wild type 426bp WT 4 Knock-out 256bp KO
creator: Hanne HD Dhondt
